{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nōhi Gokoku Shrine
濃飛護國神社 | image = | width = 250 | caption = | type = | dedication = | founded = [[1871]] | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2-55 Kuruwa-machi, [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]]
[[Gifu Prefecture]]
Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | phone = | website = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. ==History== ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Gifu Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:濃飛護國神社]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Nōhi Gokoku Shrine
濃飛護國神社 | image = | width = 250 | caption = | type = | dedication = | founded = [[1871]] | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2-55 Kuruwa-machi, [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]]
[[Gifu Prefecture]]
Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | phone = | website = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in [[1871]] to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in [[1940]]. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Visitor attractions in Gifu Prefecture]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:濃飛護國神社]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Nōhi Gokoku Shrine
濃飛護國神社 | image = | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|40.27|N|136|36|57.44|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 1871 | date_destroyed = | location = 2-55 Kuruwa-machi, [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]]
[[Gifu Prefecture]]
Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. The shrines two festival days are April 23 and September 22. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nohi Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:100 Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. The shrines two festival days are April 23 and September 22. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nohi Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:100 {{Infobox religious building | name = Nōhi Gokoku Shrine
濃飛護國神社 | image = | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|40.27|N|136|36|57.44|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 1871 | date_destroyed = | location = 2-55 Kuruwa-machi, [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]]
[[Gifu Prefecture]]
Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. The shrines two festival days are April 23 and September 22. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nohi Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:100 Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. The shrines two festival days are April 23 and September 22. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nohi Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:100 Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. The shrines two festival days are April 23 and September 22. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nohi Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:100 It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. {{Infobox religious building | name = Nōhi Gokoku Shrine
濃飛護國神社 | image = | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|40.27|N|136|36|57.44|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 1871 | date_destroyed = | location = 2-55 Kuruwa-machi, [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]]
[[Gifu Prefecture]]
Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. The shrines two festival days are April 23 and September 22. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nohi Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:100 Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[Gifu Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[Gifu, Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. 濃飛護國神社 | image = | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|21|40.27|N|136|36|57.44|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 1871 | date_destroyed = | location = 2-55 Kuruwa-machi, [[:en:Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]]
[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]
Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Nōhi Gokoku Shrine'''|濃飛護國神社|Nōhi Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is located near the base of [[:en:Ōgaki Castle|Ōgaki Castle]]. The shrine is designated to the approximately 19,000 people from the Seino and Hida regions of Gifu Prefecture who died during wars. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. The shrines two festival days are April 23 and September 22. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] }} [[en:Nōhi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fr:Nōhi Gokoku-jinja]] [[ja:濃飛護國神社]] {{wikidata link|Q2402717}} [[Category:1000 shortest pages as of Nov 16, 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally built in 1871 to honor the 54 men from the area who had died during the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. Originally, it was called '''Ōgaki Shōkonsha''' (大垣招魂社). The shrine was replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] in nearby [[:en:Gifu|Gifu]] after it was completed in 1940. The shrines two festival days are April 23 and September 22. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Hida Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Shinto-stub}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[en:Nōhi Gokoku Shrine]] [[fr:Nōhi Gokoku-jinja]] [[ja:濃飛護國神社]] {{wikidata link|Q2402717}} [[Category:1000 shortest pages as of Nov 16, 2025]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
